title: Build Jan-Dhan Gateway with employee dataset

requirements.text: Generate a new CSV dataset file at frontend/public/assets/jan_dhan_registry_2000.csv containing approximately 500 employee records with columns: employee_id (text), name (text), email (text), phone (text), department (text), designation (text), date_of_joining (DD-MM-YYYY format), salary (number), location (text), status (Active/Inactive). Include variety across departments (HR, Finance, IT, Operations, Marketing, Sales), multiple designations (Manager, Senior Executive, Executive, Team Lead), various locations (Mumbai, Delhi, Bangalore, Chennai, Hyderabad, Pune), mix of Active/Inactive status, salaries ranging from 25000 to 150000, and joining dates between 2018 and 2024.

quotes: now create a same website with this dataset

acceptanceCriteria: CSV file contains approximately 500 employee records
All columns from uploaded dataset are preserved: employee_id, name, email, phone, department, designation, date_of_joining, salary, location, status
Data includes variety of departments, designations, locations, and status values
Salary values range between 25000 and 150000
Date of joining values span from 2018 to 2024
File is accessible for download at frontend/public/assets/jan_dhan_registry_2000.csv

requirements.text: Update backend data model in backend/main.mo to support employee registry with fields: employee_id (Text), name (Text), email (Text), phone (Text), department (Text), designation (Text), date_of_joining (Text for DD-MM-YYYY format), salary (Nat), location (Text), status (Text for Active/Inactive). Replace or extend existing CitizenRecord type to accommodate employee data structure.

quotes: now create a same website with this dataset

acceptanceCriteria: Backend data model supports all employee fields from the uploaded dataset
Type definitions allow for employee_id, name, email, phone, department, designation, date_of_joining, salary, location, and status
CSV import logic can parse and store employee records

requirements.text: Update transaction processing interface in frontend/src/components/TransactionForm.tsx to work with employee records instead of citizen records. Replace Citizen_ID input with employee_id input, update scheme selection to reflect employee-relevant schemes or benefits (e.g., Salary Advance, Medical Reimbursement, Travel Allowance), and adjust validation logic to check employee eligibility based on department, designation, status, and other employee-specific criteria.

quotes: now create a same website with this dataset

acceptanceCriteria: Form accepts employee_id instead of Citizen_ID
Scheme selection dropdown includes employee-relevant benefit types
Validation displays results based on employee record attributes
Form submission processes employee data correctly

requirements.text: Update Gate 1: Eligibility Gate validation logic to check that employee_id exists in employee registry, status equals Active, department and designation match eligibility criteria for requested benefit scheme, and salary falls within appropriate range for the benefit type.

quotes: now create a same website with this dataset

acceptanceCriteria: Validation checks employee_id existence in registry
Status must be Active for approval
Department and designation are validated against benefit eligibility rules
Salary range is checked for benefit type appropriateness
Clear rejection reasons are provided when validation fails

requirements.text: Update transaction history display in frontend/src/components/TransactionHistory.tsx to show employee-specific transaction details including employee_id, employee name, department, designation, benefit type, amount, approval/rejection status, and timestamp.

quotes: now create a same website with this dataset

acceptanceCriteria: Transaction history displays employee_id instead of Citizen_ID
Employee name, department, and designation are visible in transaction records
Benefit type is clearly displayed for each transaction
Status badges show approval/rejection with appropriate styling
Timestamps are formatted consistently

requirements.text: Update application branding and labels throughout the UI to reflect employee benefit processing instead of citizen benefit processing. Change 'Jan-Dhan Gateway' header to 'Employee Benefits Gateway', update form labels to use employee terminology, and adjust all user-facing text to reflect the employee benefits context.

quotes: now create a same website with this dataset

acceptanceCriteria: Application header displays 'Employee Benefits Gateway'
Form labels use employee-specific terminology
All user-facing text reflects employee benefits context
Help text and error messages reference employees instead of citizens
Footer and metadata reflect updated application purpose

constraints: Maintain existing three-gate validation architecture (eligibility, budget, frequency)
Do not modify Internet Identity authentication hooks
Do not change Shadcn UI component sources
nonGoals: Adding real-time employee synchronization with external HR systems
